title: Rivian Production Cost Reduction Strategy 2024-2025
text: Rivian cut $31,000 in cost per vehicle compared to Q4 2023, and this momentum has continued into 2025. The company significantly reduced its automotive cost of goods sold per vehicle by $22,600 compared to Q1 2024. Rivian cut 10% of its workforce in 2024-2025 to reduce costs and improve operational efficiency. Significant progress was made in reducing material costs for the Gen 2 R1 vehicles compared to the first generation, with a projected 20% material cost reduction from Q1 2024 to Q4 2024 for a dual-motor, large-pack configuration. R2 platform aims for a 45% production cost reduction versus R1, targeting broader market adoption starting in 2026. The R2 SUV, priced 65% lower than R1S, leverages 50% cheaper production costs and scalable platform design. The partnership with Volkswagen is projected to yield over $2.25 billion in cumulative cost savings by 2026 through shared supply chains and development efficiencies. The full year of Gen 2 production and the implementation of lean manufacturing principles are expected to drive further cost reductions.
